_comment: v0.8 Tier-2 AUTHORED game-specific guides for Windows games using GameNative, GameHub, and Mobox runners. Re-authored to the canonical Windows knob set (Runner, Wine/Proton build, DX wrapper+version, DX level, Box64/FEX preset, GPU driver, CPU cores, video memory, dependencies, env vars, DRM/offline, resolution/FPS). DEVICE TARGET: AYN Odin 3 / Snapdragon 8 Elite / Adreno 830 / Android 15 â€” on GameNative the working graphics driver for Adreno 830 is 'Wrapper' with 'Use Adrenotools Turnip' ON (a recent Turnip build), matching a real exported .gamenative config (graphicsDriver=wrapper, adrenotoolsTurnip=1); 'Vortek (Universal)' and 'SD 8 Elite' are fallbacks in the same Graphics Driver dropdown. (GameHub/Mobox Winlator-derived runners still expose Vortek separately.) Runner is matched to each game's weight class: GameNative (Proton) for DX12/heavy/anti-cheat titles, GameHub for general DX11 mid-weight, Mobox (Box64+Wine, no DXVK) for DX9/2D/light titles, and DXVK-D3D9 + Box64 under GameNative for CPU-light DX9 titles. All values are Tier 2 (Authored) â€” recommended starting points that still need on-device verification; wording is intentionally honest ('recommended / try'). FPS figures are estimates.

steps.text: Install GameNative. DMC HD Collection is DirectX 9 and CPU-light, so it runs best with a DXVK-D3D9 wrapper over Box64 â€” Proton-GE is NOT required for this DX9 game.

Graphics driver on Adreno 830 / Snapdragon 8 Elite: use 'Wrapper' with 'Use Adrenotools Turnip' turned ON and a recent Turnip version (e.g. turnip25.3.0_R11) â€” this matches a real exported .gamenative config (graphicsDriver=wrapper, adrenotoolsTurnip=1). If a game won't render on Turnip, try the 'Vortek (Universal)' or 'SD 8 Elite' entries in the same Graphics Driver dropdown.

ALREADY BAKED IN â€” the downloadable config for this guide turns these Wine components ON automatically, so you do NOT need to install them by hand. They are listed only so you know what is enabled. To verify/toggle them manually: long-press the game in GameNative -> Container Settings -> Wine Components, and confirm each is set to 1 (enabled).

settings.Direct3D (direct3d=1): ON â€” installs the full d3dx9 + d3dcompiler DLL set (this IS the 'DirectX 9 / d3dx9 / DirectX June 2010' runtime; no separate install needed)
settings.XAudio (xaudio=1): ON â€” x3daudio / xactengine / xaudio2 DLLs DMC3 uses for sound
settings.DirectShow (directshow=1): ON â€” quartz / qasf filters used in the FMV playback path
settings.VC++ 2010 (vcrun2010=1): ON â€” Visual C++ 2010 runtime (the only vcrun GameNative bundles)
settings.WM Decoder (wmdecoder=1): ON â€” Windows Media (WMV) decoder DLLs; best-effort for the intro video

steps.text: Recommended container settings for DMC HD Collection (DX9 / CPU-light). Verify on-device â€” these are authored starting points.

settings.GPU driver: Wrapper + 'Use Adrenotools Turnip' ON, recent Turnip (e.g. turnip25.3.0_R11) on Adreno 830; 'Vortek (Universal)' or 'SD 8 Elite' as fallbacks
settings.DRM / online: Fully offline â€” no online or anti-cheat

steps.text: Copy your installed DMC HD Collection here, then launch the per-game exe: dmc1.exe (Devil May Cry), dmc2.exe (DMC2), or dmc3.exe (DMC3 Special Edition).

THIS IS A 3-GAME COLLECTION — dmc1.exe (Devil May Cry 1), dmc2.exe (DMC2), dmc3.exe (DMC3 Dante's Awakening) are three separate executables in the same install. The config attached to this guide defaults to dmc3.exe. To play DMC1 or DMC2: long-press the game in GameNative >> Container Settings >> General >> Executable Path >> pick dmc1.exe or dmc2.exe from the dropdown >> Save >> relaunch. Do NOT use dmcLauncher.exe — it crashes the Wine child process when you try to pick any game from its menu. The game-fix that renames .wmv files covers all three games (dmc1, dmc2, dmc3) and the launcher's attract-mode videos automatically.

DMC3 crashes right after the loading screen while LOADING its pre-rendered intro video â€” Wine's WMV3/VC-1 decode fails ('Unrecognized format WMV3'). Be aware up front: there is NO reliable launch-arg / env-var / DLL-override that skips this; the wmdecoder + directshow components are already ON here and it still crashes, which matches every Wine/Proton report. The DEPENDABLE fix is to rename the .wmv files at data\dmc3\Video\*.wmv so the game skips them (real-time in-engine cutscenes still play fine). Because you installed via Steam INSIDE GameNative, those files sit in app-private storage that NO Android file manager can reach, so the EASIEST route is: long-press the game -> 'Open container' -> open the in-container file manager -> go to A:\data\dmc3\Video -> rename each .wmv to .wmv.bak. (Fallback: turn on 'use external storage' and reinstall so a normal Android file manager can reach them.) See the first troubleshooting entry for full tap-by-tap steps.
Launch the desired title (dmc1/dmc2/dmc3) at 1080p, cap to 60 fps. Expect a smooth locked 60 once shaders are compiled â€” these are 2001-2005-era engines.
First boot compiles DXVK shaders â€” the screen may sit black or stutter for up to a minute. Wait it out; subsequent launches are fast.

DMC3 is the known problem child of this collection â€” treat this config and the steps above as fixes to TRY, not a guarantee. Once DMC3 actually boots and plays for you, export your working GameNative config and import it back into this app to mark it verified.

troubleshooting.symptom: DMC3 opens but crashes right after the loading screen (the most common DMC3 failure)
troubleshooting.fix:
This is the pre-rendered intro/title WMV. Right after the loading screen DMC3 tries to play data\dmc3\Video\*.wmv; Wine's WMV3/VC-1 (winegstreamer/mfplat) decode fails on 'Unrecognized format WMV3' and the game crashes.

HONEST ANSWER FIRST: there is NO reliable launch-arg / env-var / DLL-override that skips or tolerates this intro. The wmdecoder + directshow components are already ON in this config and the crash still happens â€” that matches every report on Wine/Proton (Proton issue #4059; wine-tkg #205; WineHQ forum t=34526). The dependable fix is to remove (rename) the intro .wmv files so the game skips them. The in-game 'M = skip cutscene' key does NOT help, because the crash happens while LOADING the intro before you ever get control. Real-time in-engine cutscenes still play fine after you do this.

WHY a plain Android file manager can't reach the files: a Steam-inside-GameNative install puts the videos in app-PRIVATE storage (/data/user/0/app.gamenative/...). On Android 13+ that folder is sandboxed â€” no Android file manager and no non-root adb can open it. So use one of the two methods below.

EASIEST â€” METHOD A: RENAME FROM INSIDE THE CONTAINER (no reinstall). In this config the game install is mapped to Windows drive A: (drives = A:\...\Devil May Cry HD Collection). Tap-by-tap: 1) In GameNative, long-press 'Devil May Cry HD Collection' and tap 'Open container' (this boots the Wine/Windows desktop WITHOUT launching the game). 2) On that desktop, open the file manager: tap the Start/menu button (bottom-left) and look for 'File Explorer' / 'winefile'. If you don't see one, tap the menu -> Shortcuts/Apps -> Add, set the executable to winefile (or explorer.exe), then run it. 3) In the file manager, open drive A:, then the folders data -> dmc3 -> Video. The full path is A:\data\dmc3\Video. 4) For each file ending in .wmv: long-press it (or two-finger tap to get the right-click menu) -> Rename -> change .wmv to .wmv.bak (rename, don't delete, so it's reversible). Do this for every .wmv in that folder. 5) Tap the menu -> Exit/Close to leave the container, then back in GameNative launch dmc3.exe. It now skips the missing FMVs and boots.

FALLBACK â€” METHOD B: MAKE THE FILES REACHABLE FROM ANDROID (reinstall to external). 1) GameNative -> Settings -> Downloads & Storage -> turn ON 'use external storage'. ('No external storage detected' only means no SD card/USB is plugged in; internal still works.) 2) Move/reinstall DMC HD Collection to external. 3) Files now appear at /storage/emulated/0/Android/data/app.gamenative/files/Steam/steamapps/common/Devil May Cry HD Collection/data/dmc3/Video, where any Android file manager can rename each .wmv to .wmv.bak. 4) Relaunch dmc3.exe.

LAST RESORT (only if you want the cutscenes to actually PLAY): on a PC, transcode the .wmv to H.264 but keep the .wmv extension (ffmpeg -i in.wmv -c:v libx264 out.wmv), then push them back via Method B. This is extra work and not needed just to get the game running.

troubleshooting.symptom: DMC1 or DMC2 crashes right after the loading screen / on the title screen
troubleshooting.fix: Same root cause as DMC3: Wine cannot decode the pre-rendered .wmv intro videos for DMC1 (data\dmc1\Video) or DMC2 (data\dmc2\Video). The game-fix in this config already renames those files to .wmv.bak automatically on launch. If you are still seeing the crash, the fix may not have run yet — try closing and relaunching the game once. If it persists: open the container (long-press > Open container), open the file manager, navigate to A:\data\dmc1\Video (or dmc2\Video), and rename each .wmv to .wmv.bak manually.

troubleshooting.symptom: Any game (DMC1/DMC2/DMC3) crashes the instant you pick it from the collection menu — dmcLauncher issue (before any loading screen)
troubleshooting.fix: You are launching the collection's wrapper (dmcLauncher.exe), which crashes the Wine child process when you try to pick any game from its menu (DMC1, DMC2, or DMC3). Always launch the per-game exe directly. The config attached to this guide already launches dmc3.exe directly, so re-import it. If you set the executable by hand: long-press the game in GameNative -> Container Settings (or Edit) -> find the Executable Path field -> pick dmc1.exe, dmc2.exe, or dmc3.exe from the dropdown (NOT dmcLauncher.exe) -> Save -> relaunch.

troubleshooting.symptom: Still crashes at boot / 'application error' before the menu appears
troubleshooting.fix: NO ACTION NEEDED FOR DIRECTX â€” this config auto-installs the DirectX 9 runtime. In GameNative, direct3d=1 installs the full d3dx9 + d3dcompiler DLL set (that IS the 'DirectX June 2010 / d3dx9' runtime; GameNative has no separate d3dx9 button). To confirm it is on: long-press the game -> Container Settings -> Wine Components -> check that Direct3D and VC++ 2010 are enabled (set to 1) -> Save. If you imported the attached config they are already on.

troubleshooting.symptom: Black screen / GPU init crash at launch on Snapdragon 8 Elite (Adreno 830)
troubleshooting.fix: Switch the Graphics Driver to Wrapper + Adrenotools Turnip on Adreno 830. Steps: 1) Long-press DMC HD Collection in GameNative. 2) Tap Container Settings (or Edit Container). 3) Open the Graphics Driver dropdown. 4) Pick 'Wrapper' and turn ON 'Use Adrenotools Turnip', then choose a recent Turnip under Graphics Driver Version (e.g. turnip25.3.0_R11). 5) Tap Save/Apply. 6) Relaunch dmc3.exe. If it still won't render, try the 'Vortek (Universal)' or 'SD 8 Elite' entries in that same dropdown.

troubleshooting.symptom: Game renders wrong / glitches / DX9 rendering issues
troubleshooting.fix: Try WineD3D instead of DXVK for this DX9 title. Steps: 1) Long-press the game -> Container Settings. 2) Open the DX Wrapper (dxwrapper) setting. 3) Switch it from DXVK to WineD3D -> Save -> relaunch. If you prefer to stay on DXVK, instead pick the newest bundled DXVK version in that same DX Wrapper dropdown and relaunch.

troubleshooting.symptom: Black screen with NO audio at launch
troubleshooting.fix: Almost always the GPU driver on this device. Confirm the Graphics Driver is 'Wrapper' with 'Use Adrenotools Turnip' ON: long-press the game -> Container Settings -> Graphics Driver -> select Wrapper + Adrenotools Turnip (recent Turnip version) -> Save -> relaunch. If it still won't render, try the 'Vortek (Universal)' or 'SD 8 Elite' entries in the same dropdown.

troubleshooting.symptom: Black screen on cutscenes but audio still plays
troubleshooting.fix: This config already enables the audio/codec components (vcrun2010, xaudio, directshow, wmdecoder all set to 1), so first just try launching â€” the WMV decoder may let the FMV play. If a pre-rendered FMV still blanks the screen, make DMC3 skip it by renaming the offending .wmv in data\dmc3\Video to .wmv.bak. An Android file manager CANNOT see the default Steam-internal copy of those files, so reach them the same two ways as the top troubleshooting entry: (A) open the container ('Open container') and use the in-container Windows file manager (Winlator File Manager / winefile / explorer.exe) to rename them on the mapped game drive (A: in this config); or (B) in Settings -> Downloads & Storage turn ON 'use external storage' (if it says 'No external storage detected', that just means no physical SD card/USB is plugged in â€” modern builds still use /storage/emulated/0/Android/data/app.gamenative/files/...), then move/reinstall the game so the files appear at /storage/emulated/0/Android/data/app.gamenative/files/Steam/steamapps/common/Devil May Cry HD Collection/data/dmc3/Video where a normal Android file manager can rename them. Then relaunch.

troubleshooting.symptom: In-game (real-time) black screen with audio playing
troubleshooting.fix: DXVK rendering issue, not a video file. Long-press the game -> Container Settings -> DX Wrapper -> either pick the newest bundled DXVK version, or switch to WineD3D -> Save -> relaunch.

troubleshooting.symptom: Game won't start at all / exits immediately
troubleshooting.fix: Two things to try, one at a time. (A) Box64 preset: long-press the game -> Container Settings -> Box64 -> set the preset to Compatibility instead of Performance -> Save -> relaunch. (B) Env var: in the same Container Settings open Environment Variables and add MESA_EXTENSION_MAX_YEAR=2003 -> Save -> relaunch.
